novel_features.name: Tonight Picker
novel_features.description: Pick what to watch tonight from trending titles actually streaming on your services.
novel_features.rationale: Joins TMDb trending + discover with per-title watch providers filtered to your --providers set in your --region — no existing CLI does the streaming-availability filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs a streaming-filtered shortlist; one call replaces tab-bouncing across TMDb/RT/JustWatch.

novel_features.name: Multi-Source Ratings Card
novel_features.description: TMDb + IMDb + Rotten Tomatoes + Metacritic ratings for any title in one card.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-source aggregation: TMDb has streaming and cast; OMDb has RT/Metacritic. Joined via IMDb ID — no single endpoint exposes all four.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs the canonical multi-source rating for a title; degrades gracefully to TMDb-only if OMDB_API_KEY is unset.

novel_features.name: Marathon Planner
novel_features.description: Plan a franchise marathon with watch order, total runtime, and suggested breaks.
novel_features.rationale: Resolves title → belongs_to_collection → /collection/{id}, normalizes order, sums runtimes, inserts breakpoints. No single API call returns a planned marathon.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when planning an event watch; agent can dump the schedule to share with a group.

novel_features.name: Career Timeline
novel_features.description: Explore any actor or director's full filmography with ratings and chronology.
novel_features.rationale: Person credits + per-title detail fan-out + OMDb ratings; sorted chronologically with role/year/runtime/rating.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs a rated chronological filmography; replaces flat IMDb lists with cross-source ratings.

novel_features.name: Watchlist
novel_features.description: Local SQLite watchlist; flag rows that are streamable on your services.
novel_features.rationale: Local store + per-row /watch/providers fan-out + filter against user --providers set. No public CLI tells you what on your watchlist is streamable now.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use weekly to surface streamable items from a saved list; eliminates ad-hoc JustWatch checks per title.

novel_features.name: Head-to-Head Versus
novel_features.description: Compare two movies or shows side-by-side across ratings, cast, runtime, and streaming.
novel_features.rationale: Composes Multi-Source Ratings Card across two titles + cast intersection + provider lists per region; aligned columnar output.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent has to pick between two finalists; one command shows where they differ on every axis.

novel_features.name: Recommendation Queue
novel_features.description: Suggest next-watch picks derived from your watchlist's recommendations and similars.
novel_features.rationale: For every row in local watchlist: union /recommendations + /similar, dedupe, rank by vote_average × log(vote_count).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs a fresh queue derived from saved interests; combines local state with API recommendations.

novel_features.name: Recurring Collaborators
novel_features.description: List people who appear in 2+ of a person's credits, with count and titles.
novel_features.rationale: Person combined_credits + per-title /credits, group-by name + count. No single endpoint surfaces this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent is researching a filmmaker's circle; surfaces recurring DPs/composers/actors mechanically.

narrative.headline: The movie CLI that combines TMDb's discovery engine with OMDb's multi-source ratings — and ships a SQLite watchlist that flags what's streaming on your services right now.
narrative.value_prop: Movie Goat unifies the workflows that today require four browser tabs: discovery (TMDb), multi-source ratings (TMDb + IMDb + RT + Metacritic via OMDb), where-to-watch (TMDb watch providers), and a local watchlist that knows your streaming services. Flagship transcendence: `tonight` picks something well-rated that's actually streaming on your services; `ratings` shows the four canonical scores for any title; `marathon` totals runtime across a franchise.
narrative.auth_narrative: TMDb v3 API key (free, https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api) is required and goes in `TMDB_API_KEY` — used as a query parameter, not a Bearer header. OMDb key (free, http://www.omdbapi.com/apikey.aspx) is optional and goes in `OMDB_API_KEY`; without it, `ratings` and `versus` show TMDb-only and gracefully omit the IMDb/RT/Metacritic columns.

quickstart.comment: The fastest path to the value: a streaming-filtered, well-rated shortlist for tonight.
Multi-type search across movies, TV, and people.
Cross-source rating card for Fight Club (TMDb id 550).
Add Inception to the local SQLite watchlist.
Show which saved titles are streamable on your services right now.
Plan a franchise marathon with totalled runtime.

troubleshoots.symptom: 401 Unauthorized: "Invalid API key: You must be granted a valid key."
troubleshoots.fix: TMDb v3 keys (32-char hex) must be sent as a query parameter, not as a Bearer header. The CLI does this automatically — re-check that TMDB_API_KEY is set to the v3 key from https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api, not a v4 read access token.

troubleshoots.symptom: `ratings` shows only the TMDb score and skips IMDb/RT/Metacritic columns.
troubleshoots.fix: Set OMDB_API_KEY to a free key from http://www.omdbapi.com/apikey.aspx. Without it the CLI degrades to TMDb-only by design.

troubleshoots.symptom: `watchlist list --available` returns rows but every row says `not available`.
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--region` (e.g. `--region US`) and confirm `--providers` matches TMDb's provider names — run `movie-goat-pp-cli providers` to see the canonical list for your region.

troubleshoots.symptom: 429 Too Many Requests when running `career` or `marathon` on long filmographies/collections.
troubleshoots.fix: TMDb's rate limit is ~40 req/10s. The CLI's adaptive limiter ramps down on 429 automatically; rerun, and the ceiling will be discovered. For very long careers, use `--since YEAR` to scope the fan-out.

narrative.when_to_use: Use Movie Goat when an agent needs to answer cinephile questions that require combining streaming availability with multi-source ratings. It is the right choice for tonight-picker scenarios, franchise marathon planning, and rated career timelines. It is not the right choice for box-office tracking, review sentiment analysis, or any workflow that needs LLM-style summaries of plot or reviews.

recipes.title: Tonight, well-rated, on my services
recipes.explanation: Streaming-filtered shortlist with only the high-gravity fields an agent needs to decide.

recipes.title: Watchlist sweep
recipes.explanation: Weekly check: which saved titles became streamable on services I have.

recipes.title: Rated career deep dive
recipes.explanation: Agent-bounded chronological filmography with the cross-source rating columns that matter.

recipes.title: Pick between two finalists
recipes.explanation: Aligned compare card for Inception vs. Tenet; ratings, runtime, cast overlap, providers.

recipes.title: Plan a franchise night
recipes.explanation: Ordered watchlist with total runtime and break suggestions.
